The Waypoint TV App is the world's leading entertainment destination for the outdoors, featuring the ultimate collection of fishing, hunting, and outdoor adventure programming.

localizedStrings.text: Chasin' The Sun
A fishing and lifestyle series highlighting the amazing diversity in and around the emerald gulf waters of Panama City Beach, FL.
Chasin' The Sun Season 11
Chasin' The Sun Season 11
Chasin' The Sun S11:E1 "Positive = Good Day"
When Justin and Meredith stay positive after a series of bad breaks, it pays off in fun. If fun means fish. After all, we are in Panama City Beach.
Chasin' The Sun S11:E2 "We’re gonna need a  bigger cooler."

Florida WaterSports Jeff Lange & Capt Justin head out of PCB in the wee morning hours hoping to land a limit of tasty table fare before the window of opportunity closes. Pro tip: Electric reels save shoulders.
Chasin' The Sun S11:E3 "Live Fishing Report"

PCB Fishing report author Nathan Chennaux joins Capt. Justin 
scanning the beach waters for tarpon and hooks into a monster while maneuvering around storms and vacationers. Does it get to the boat?
Chasin' The Sun S11:E4 "Black Pearl’s Final  Resting Place"

Joined by PCB’s Artificial Reef Coordinator Erin Graham Capt Justin 
fish previously dropped reefs before sinking the famed BLACK PEARL from iconic films right off Panama City Beach, FL.
Chasin' The Sun S11:E5 "Tarpon Telegraph"
We listen to guides and anglers on how they attempt to hook into the migrating tarpon that pass thru PCB’s beautiful beach waters each summer. It’s a lesson in humility, respect and yes....luck.
Chasin' The Sun Season 10
Chasin' The Sun Season 10
Chasin' The Sun S10:E1 "Red to Black to Jack"
Capt. Justin Leake and a fellow guide search for redfish along the beach, but Mother Nature has other ideas and gives them a show they didn’t expect.
Chasin' The Sun S10:E2 "Beach For Fun Reef For Flavor"

For Capt. Justin Leake, sight fishing the beach is one of the most productive, and FUN ways to fish. When that doesn’t work, Capt. Todd Jones suggests fishing an artificial reef, and as it turns out, that’s where dinner lives.
Chasin' The Sun S10:E3 "First Things First"
Meredith McCord arrives in late spring and joins Capt. Justin Leake on the hunt for a fish she’s never caught before. Lo and behold, it’s a day fit for a king.
Chasin' The Sun S10:E4 "Tarpon Producer"
With Meredith McCord halfway around the world, Capt. Justin Leake gives show producer Chris Hermans his first shot at landing a tarpon after filming them for 9 seasons. Does art imitate life?
Chasin' The Sun S10:E5 "Billfish Tourney"

Capt. Justin Leake joins some old friends on board the Fall Line in hopes of scoring big in the Bay Point Billfish Open. This trip will test the limits of all the anglers as the skilled captain and crew do the impossible in hopes of bringing home the prize.
Chasin' The Sun S10:E6 "Electric Deep Drop"
Capt. Justin and Florida WaterSports pro Jeff Lange go deep off Panama City Beach to test the limits of a new battery powered reel. The results are surprising.
Chasin' The Sun S10:E7 "Once Upon a Wahoo"
When tarpon don’t cooperate, it’s time for Capt. Justin and friend Ethan Register to have a competition for dinner options in the gulf’s grocery store.
Chasin' The Sun Season 9
Chasin' The Sun Season 9
Chasin' The Sun S9:E1 "Tarpon Town"

PCB’s white sand beaches are jam-packed with visitors soaking up sun
and surf while Justin and Meredith chase schools of migrating tarpon
just offshore. How often do those two worlds collide?
Chasin' The Sun S9:E2 "Silver King Thing"

While releasing a defeated tarpon healthy after an epic battle is the ultimate victory, everything else is measured in heartbreak and degrees of failure. For Justin, putting Ethan on his first silver king requires patience and a tasty diversion by a local chef.
Chasin' The Sun S9:E3 "Flounder To Slam"
Meredith learns the value of the Trash Can Slam on a flounder trip with Capt. Justin that turns into a multi-species challenge.
Chasin' The Sun S9:E4 "Dog Daze Delight"

Capt Justin Leake and Meredith McCord divert attention from what’s not happening with the tarpon bite to using CoastMaster, light tackle and experience to find snapper success on heavily fished public spots.
Chasin' The Sun S9:E5 "Billfish Open Rebirth"
When the Bay Point Billfish Open returns, we hop on board the Ellie B for some offshore competition and follow the event’s epic dockside party.
Chasin' The Sun S9:E6 "A Bridge Too Red"
Capt Justin & Meredith’s beautiful Panama City Beach morning is met with a slack tide so a new path to redfish success is found under a popular bridge.
Chasin' The Sun S9:E7 "Dream Boat Build"
If you had a blank check to build the best fishing boat possible, what would you create? Capt. Justin gets his wish, but it doesn’t come quickly or easily.
Chasin' The Sun S9:E8 "Fishing the Dream Boat"
Capt. Justin takes a fellow guide on his new boat to see how it measures up. Using only public waypoints on his Humminbird unit, they find exploring to be as much fun as it is fruitful.
Chasin' The Sun S9:E9 "Happy Returns"
Capt. Justin heads offshore, dodges storms and shows how the "Return 'Em Right" program is saving fish that experience barotrauma.
Chasin' The Sun S9:E10 "Reef Dropping and Diving For All"

We follow along as a decommissioned ship is sunk to create a new artificial reef, much to the delight of divers and anglers. Plus, Adapt-Able SCUBA makes underwater adventure possible for those who didn’t 
think it was.
Chasin' The Sun Season 8
Chasin' The Sun Season 8
Chasin' The Sun S8:E1 "Happy Head Games"

A beautiful beach day and confident anglers is the perfect scenario to prove tarpon never play by the rules.  Justin & Meredith get a dose of tarpon psychology and begin to second guess their angling skills.
Chasin' The Sun S8:E2 "Hook Lessons"
Justin and Meredith return to the chase after taking a lesson from finicky tarpon. This time, they pay close attention to how hooks and presentations affect their odds of a strike.
Chasin' The Sun S8:E3 "Lucky Fish"

Capt. Todd Jones jumps on Justin’s boat for a sight fishing dream day. After success along the beach, they turn to handlining for Goliath grouper and witness the amazing survival saga of the world’s luckiest fish.
Chasin' The Sun S8:E4 "Locals Day"
Justin entices a friend to play hooky from work to set hooks offshore on a perfect “locals” day. The only thing better than the weather was the dinner they caught and cooked.
Chasin' The Sun S8:E5 "Epic Redfish"

Witness a truly epic day on the water when Capt. Justin and Meredith find a secluded slice of beach teeming with redfish biting virtually everything. Topwater plugs, rusty flies and a live well full of bait make for an experience no redfish angler would forget
Chasin' The Sun S8:E6 "Best Go With Plan B"

When your gut tells you to do something different than what’s in front of you, do you listen?

Capt. Justin and Meredith listen, and find that success comes in many forms. And it turns out that success can be really tasty, too!
Chasin' The Sun S8:E7 "Grouper Open"

It’s opening day of grouper season and, for Capt. Justin Leake and one of his longtime fishing buddies, that means a trip offshore is in order. And what better way to break in a new boat than heading to some reefs and filling the cooler with one of the Gulf of Mexico’s most popular fish?
Chasin' The Sun S8:E8 "Foggy Bottom Fishing"

There’s nothing like a change in weather conditions to wreck a perfectly good fishing plan, and that’s exactly what Capt. Justin Leake and his cousin encounter when December turns unseasonably warm. It’s a grey morning with thick fog so Justin and Capt. Brent Berry won’t be heading offshore right away as planned. Instead, Justin turns to his Humminbird CoastMaster charts to pinpoint a near shore artificial reef just five minutes from the St. Andrews Pass, where the anglers will do a little fishing until the fog burns off. Capt. Leake opts to send a jig to the bottom and he’s not very picky about the one that gets tied on. “When fishing reefs, the specific jig doesn’t matter because it’s all about the presentation,” he says. “When you’re sitting on top of a wreck, break out the ‘junk jigs’ – they get bit just fine.” And that proves to be the case as Justin and Brent enjoy non-stop battles against amberjack, red snapper and bonita. And with a bite this good, there’s no reason to go anywhere else! Also, we take a look at the United States Coast Guard Auxiliary’s new paddle craft safety initiative.
Chasin' The Sun S8:E9 "Offshore Limp"

We return to the Ellie B and head a hundred miles offshore to repeat last year’s epic tuna bite. But someone must have brought a banana on board because the plan suddenly turned into an unexpected adventure.
Chasin' The Sun S8:E10 "Offshore Schooling"
Capt. Justin and some good ol’ boys get a lesson in angling skills when the captain's wife takes them to school during a trip offshore.
Chasin' The Sun S8:E11 "100 Shows Thousands of Fish"
In our 100th episode we look back and detail a true labor of love. Discussing the fun and challenges learning and adapting to fishing & filming the amazing fishery surrounding Panama City Beach.
Chasin' The Sun Season 7
Chasin' The Sun Season 7
Chasin' The Sun S7:E1 "Travis & Beyond"
We celebrate the great times we had with the late Capt. Travis Holeman, then look to the future with a renewed passion for adventure and feature highlights from the upcoming season.
Chasin' The Sun S7:E2 "Unconventional Tarpon"

Fly-fishing master Meredith McCord joins Capt. Justin Leake and uses unfamiliar tackle to chase tarpon along the beach. Can the veteran fly angler land a silver king on conventional gear? The answer is spectacular!
Chasin' The Sun S7:E3 "Fish On Smack"
Capt. Justin Leake takes redfish tournament pro Fred Meyers out of his comfort zone to talk trash and chase tarpon just a few hundred yards off a lively Panama City Beach.
Chasin' The Sun S7:E4 "Chasin' a World Record Part 1"

Capt. Justin Leake and champion angler Meredith McCord explore public waypoints and attempt to land a world record amberjack on light line. Panama City Beach’s backyard is the site of an epic battle in the first of two amazing episodes.
Chasin' The Sun S7:E5 "Chasin' a World Record Part 2"

Renowned angler Meredith McCord and Capt. Justin Leake continue their quest for a line class world record. When struggles begin the moment they leave the dock, grit and lady luck ensure an epic day ends in grand style.
Chasin' the Sun S7:E6 "Head Boat Bonanza"

When Capt. Justin Leake attempts to blend in with the crowd aboard a local head boat, he finds friends, talented deckhands and a heck of a good time. Then we take Local Phil Chester to experience a Tarpon shoulder work out.
Chasin' The Sun S7:E7 "Watercraft Fitting Guide"

Learn what you need to ask yourself before buying a watercraft. Offshore to the flats, Capt. Justin’s lifetime of buying, selling and rigging boats will help you find what best fits your needs on the water.
Chasin' The Sun S7:E8 "Deep Water Delight"

Capt Justin Leake goes back to his roots as a mate on a sportfisher and heads offshore with a group of old and new friends. They fight yellowfin tuna, marlin and monster mahi-mahi in perfect December weather on the gulf.
Chasin' The Sun S7:E9 "Reel Reef"

Constructing and launching an offshore reef takes a skilled team of pros, time, money and a little help from Mother Nature to pull off. We follow along with a University of Florida team as they repurpose enormous cable reels into reefs that will attract a variety of tasty fish for decades to come.
Chasin' The Sun  S7:E10 "Off & On Redfish"
Meredith McCord returns to PCB and joins Capt. Justin for one of his favorite way to fish -- sight fishing redfish on the beach. But it’s never easy when it’s breezy!
Chasin' The Sun S7:E11 "Rig Reason"

What makes you a better angler? It’s all in the details. Capt. Justin, Meredith McCord and Jeff Lange from Florida WaterSports go through the why, how and what to rig for different scenarios that bring success.
Chasin' The Sun S7:E12 "Flats Man Do Challenge"
A little friendly competition helps improve your skills, so Justin challenges the team from Florida WaterSports to see who comes up with the best rigs and strategy to land shallow water redfish.
Chasin' The Sun Season 6
Chasin' The Sun Season 6
Chasin' The Sun S6:E1 "Hometown Tarpon"

Justin invites life long PCB resident Griff Griffiths onboard and reveals the silver king migration is happening right in the midst all the beach-goers activities. Then ultimately landing his first local tarpon right on the beach he grew up on.
Chasin' The Sun S6:E2 "Sightfishing the WMBB"

It’s no secret that sight fishing clear emerald green water is a favorite of local anglers when conditions are perfect. But perfection is rare. Justin & Travis share methods and techniques for finding and catching fish when the conditions are less than perfect on the beach and in the bay.
Chasin' The Sun S6:E3 "Depth of Red"

What fish can you chase 365 days a year in almost any weather conditions in deep, shallow, skinny, stained or crystal clear water with a variety of baits and tactics? Justin & Travis illustrate how the Redfish is the go to sport fish that they will never get tired of chasing.
Chasin' The Sun S6:E4 "Snapper Grand Slam"

Justin has Humminbird’s Bill Carson onboard to show him how the new CoastMaster charts look in Panama City Beach waters and find it’s not just red snapper that populate these waters. It’s a smorgasbord of snapper for the cooler.
Chasin' The Sun S6:E5 "Micro Skinny Red"
When overnight temps go low and the tides go lower, it forces Redfish to move. Justin & Travis break out a new micro skiff to see how low they can go to ambush shifting redfish on the flats.
Chasin' The Sun S6:E6 "Offshore Heritage"
It’s playtime for Travis & Justin when offshore guide Josh Sauls shares a secret honey hole and his fishing ancestry. Then luck shines when the guys find another sweet spot on the way back.
Chasin' The Sun S6:E7 "Random Acts of Fishing"

The irresistibly serene gulf waters off Panama City Beach lure Justin into taking Capt. Ed Zyak out to see what's biting.  It quickly turns into a study session and a lesson.  Patience + perseverance = success.
Chasin' The Sun S6:E8 "Finding the Right Boat for You"

Justin has spent a lifetime buying, selling and rigging boats to fit his angling needs. Learn what you need to ask yourself before shopping for the perfect boat and what to look for at the dealership.
Chasin' The Sun S6:E9 "The Bottom Knows"
Determining where fish species congregate starts at the bottom. Justin and Travis examine the expansion of artificial structure and live bottom fishing and learn there's much more to be discovered.
Chasin' The Sun S6:E10 "Trick Line & Sinker"
Justin and Travis review clips from previous trips and guess the fish being targeted by what's shown on screen. We learn why they use a variety of tackle and how angling experience leads to insight.
Chasin' The Sun S6:E11 "Forward Thinking Back Part 1"
Justin and Travis look back at what they have loved and learned from the trips they have fished and filmed together.
Chasin' The Sun S6:E12 "Forward Thinking Back Part 2"

Justin and Travis continue discussing what they have learned while fishing the waters off Panama City Beach.  Also, a feature on the Panama City Beach Parks & Recreation Department's Youth Fishing Rodeo.
Winchester Life

It's a lifestyle carried on by generations around the world. Winchester Life shares the stories of the people that have made the shooting sports and hunting lifestyle a part of their everyday lives, and the places that embody the spirit of living a Winchester life.
Winchester Life Season 5
Winchester Life Season 5
Winchester Life S5:E1 "Camp Longbeard"

Spring in Michigan means turkey season is on. In this episode of Winchester Life, the crew returns for Turkey Camp 2025, where changing weather, early mornings, and vocal birds set the tone for the week. After dialing in their setups at the range, the team heads into the field to chase gobblers across hardwoods and open farmland. From patient sits at sunrise to midday moves and carefully planned decoy strategies, each hunt brings new challenges and opportunities. Close encounters, tough conditions, and well-timed shots highlight the grind of spring turkey hunting, as the crew makes the most of every chance in the Michigan woods.
Winchester Life S5:E2 "Alaska Supreme"

In this episode of Winchester Life, the crew travels to Kodiak Island, Alaska, in pursuit of blacktail deer in some of the most remote terrain they’ve faced. After a multi-day journey by air
and sea, they arrive in a landscape defined by steep mountains, thick brush, and ever-changing weather. Once on the ground, the team confirms their setups and puts Winchester’s Supreme Long Range ammunition to the test as they glass expansive hillsides, pick apart coastal terrain, and carefully plan each move. From quick opportunities along the shoreline to calculated stalks in the high country, every moment demands focus and precision. Against the backdrop of Alaska’s wild beauty, this episode highlights the challenge, the strategy, and the performance it takes to capitalize when the opportunity finally comes together.
Winchester Life S5:E3 "Precision Rifle Perfected"

The team at Winchester Ammunition has arrived in Oregon to visit the Leupold Long Range Academy. There, they’ll put Winchester’s new “Supreme Long Range” ammunition to the test, pushing its performance across demanding long-range scenarios.
 
With precision, consistency, and confidence on the line, the team is eager to demonstrate exactly what “Precision Rifle Perfected” means when every shot counts.
Winchester Life S5:E4 "The Spies Tradition"

Join Benny Spies of Team Winchester as he shares stories of his upbringing in South Dakota. From cherished family photos to duck seasons of the past, learn how a passion for hunting was ignited and became a way of life for Benny and his family.
Winchester Life S5:E5 "The Best of Winchester Life"
A "Best of..." episode spanning the past 4 seasons of Winchester Life.
Winchester Life Season 4
Winchester Life Season 4
Winchester Life S4:E1 "Longbeard Mitten Mayhem"

Over a decade after the game changing Longbeard XR was introduced by Winchester, the wait is finally over for their next ground breaking turkey load. After years of perfecting the recipe behind the scenes, Winchester finally unveiled the new Longbeard TSS at a turkey camp in northern Michigan. First watch the jaw dropping Longbeard TSS pattern board testing, then see it go to work on some spring gobblers.
Winchester Life S4:E2 "Remembering A Legend"

Nestled in the hills of Ohio just west of the Appalachians is a very special deer camp ripe with memories from deer season’s past. With the development of the 350 Legend straight-walled cartridge by Winchester in 2019, a world of opportunities now existed in states like Ohio and Michigan. In this episode, with the 350 Legend in hand, three mature whitetails meet their maker, from the same blind, over the course of three seasons.
Winchester Life S4:E3 "Six Decades Of Dominance"

It’s been 60 years since AA was introduced to the shooting sports world. Since that day, an immeasurable number of AA hulls have hit the ground and countless shooters have stood center stage raising a trophy where millions of dollars have been won. Professional and recreational shooters alike have trusted AA for six decades. In this episode, load up for stories of high school, collegiate competitors and the world’s best as they bust clays with the legendary shot shell.

GUESTS: Zach Keenbaum (Team Winchester) Desi Edmunds (Team Winchester) Kayla Wilgus (Team Winchester) Amy Cawley (Team Winchester) Anthony Matarese (Team Winchester)
Winchester Life S4:E4 "Clays With Purpose"

For the first time in history, the World English Sporting Clays Championship was held in the United States at the incredible M&M Hunting Preserve owned by the Matarese family in New Jersey. Watch as Winchester’s best shooters vie for Gold as they compete against over 2,500 shooters and 17 countries. We’ll also join the Kids and Clays foundation in Florida as they host an event that will most certainly touch your soul.
Winchester Life S4:E5 "Decade Of Deer Season"

Across the country, deer season to many Americans is a rite of passage. For generations, Winchester has been the trusted ammunition choice for hunters…and it’s been a decade since the game-changing Deer Season XP whitetail line was introduced. In this episode, we’ll celebrate the many successes by Team Winchester hunters in the field during whitetail and mule deer firearm seasons across the United States. 
GUESTS: Melissa Bachman (Team Winchester) Mark Drury (Team Winchester) Nikki Boxler (Team Winchester) Chris Keefer (Team Winchester) Casey Keefer (Team Winchester) Jason Gilbertson (Team Winchester)
Winchester Life S4:E6 "Shooting United"

The mission of Shoot United is to be a content resource for millions of shooters and a place where people can learn, build confidence and find ways to participate. Through films, educational videos and articles, SU has impacted countless shooters all over the world. In this episode, join Shoot United Ambassadors as they inspire and educate through a series of instructional videos and stories in pistol, self-defense, trap and skeet and sporting clays.   
GUESTS: Thea Van Sync (Team Winchester) Callie Farill (Team Winchester) Becky Yackley (Team Winchester) Theo Ribbs (Team Winchester) Jessica Hook (Team Winchester)
Winchester Life S4:E7 "Flight Proven"

Illinois isn’t the first state you think about when it comes to waterfowl, but the Wentz Brothers have dialed in some honey holes over the years. On this episode, a slew of waterfowl experts put to the test some of the newest Winchester waterfowl ammunition during the great migration of the Mississippi Flyway in early December. We’ll also head north to the shores of Kodiak Island, Alaska with Chris and Casey Keefer as they push the limits of Winchester’s new Last Call Double Shot on fast-ripping sea ducks.
GUESTS: Zach Wentz (Team Winchester) Nathan Robinson (Winchester Ammunition) Ryan Swaner (Winchester Repeating Arms) Chris Jennings (Ducks Unlimited) Mike Brasher (Ducks Unlimited) Scott Olmsted (American Hunter) Travis Hall (Field & Stream) Chris Keefer (Team Winchester) Casey Keefer (Team Winchester)
Winchester Life S4:E8 "The Colonel & The Olympian"

Col. Nick Nichols from Folds of Honor and Olympic skeet shooter Dania Vizzi are both proud Americans who have represented their country overseas. Col. Nichols commanded the air in the cockpit of a fighter jet for countless missions during his seven combat tours and Dania donned the stars and bars proudly on her shooting vest as an Olympian in Paris. Now with Winchesters in hand, both are in the Midwest whitetail woods during late November during the back end of the rut hoping to connect on a Missouri monarch.
Winchester Life Season 3
Winchester Life Season 3
Winchester Life S3:E1 "Midwest Monarchs"

On opening day of Missouri’s gun season, hunters from around the state seek ridges and box blinds throughout the “Show Me” state in search of mature whitetail bucks. For Nikki Boxler and the Winchester Deer Camp, it’s no different. With Winchester’s in hand, Nikki and crew play cat and mouse with several mature bucks at Indian Hill Outfitters in Pike County.
Winchester Life S3:E2 “Lovesick Longbeards”

Every season, just like clockwork, mission-driven longboards gobble their way across the plains of South Dakota, the cornfields of Iowa, the timbered ridges of Michigan and the cattle ground of Kansas looking for their soulmates in one of the most celebrated hunting seasons in America…the wild turkey breeding season. Join Team Winchester as they stalk, call, flank and roost boss gobblers into range from coast to coast.
Winchester Life S3:E3 “Lonestar Legends”

Everything is bigger in Texas, including the stories. With the whitetail rut in the midwest winding down, the Winchester Life Crew of Chris Keefer, Casey Keefer, Nikki Boxler and Paul Sawyer head south to the Texas Panhandle with Winchesters in hand hoping to wrangle in a few Texas-sized giants.
Winchester Life S3:E4 “Shooting is Life”

The positive impact made by the shooting sports and recreational shooting to people across our country can’t be measured in just one story, so we have three. In Florida, Olympian Dania Vizzi trains at her home shooting facility and talk about how she went from ballerina to one of the best shotgun shooters in the world. In Nebraska, firearms instructor Thea Van Syoc has used the disciplines of competitive pistol shooting and training to help shape her life. Finally we’ll take a look into the National African American Gun Association and learn how one day at the range for Phillip Smith created the fastest growing firearm organization in the United States.
Winchester Life S3:E5 “New Mexico Magic”

Nikki Boxler’s life in Update New York on her family farm raising cattle and producing maple syrup is a few thousand miles from the hills of New Mexico. We’ll take a look into Nikki’s life back home and then follow along as she visits the small town of Quemado and its bustling population of 228, while she pursues bugling elk with her favorite Winchester in hand. We’ll also head to Missouri and Texas to chase whitetails with Chris Keefer and Paul Sawyer.
Winchester Life S3:E6 “The Legend of Stuttgart”

We set out to tell the tale of Marion - and his legendary Mack’s Prairie Wings, smack dab in the middle of the world famous Stuttgart, Arkansas - the land of  ducks and rice. Get an inside look at a man that many consider to be larger than life, from those that knew him best, like his cousin and fellow hell raiser, Bud McCollum. We’ll even hear a story or two from Milton Stovesand, an 81 year old former waterfowl guide and Mack’s employee who actually guided John Olin a time or two, and ran the timber holes with Marion. It’s timber holes and tall tales. It’s cups of coffee and cupped up wings. It’s partners in business, and partners in potholes and it’s pure Arkansas...just like Marion.
Winchester Life S3:E7 “Big Game Bachman”

Melissa Bachman has made her name over the last two decades as one of the most dedicated hunters and producers in the outdoor industry. In this episode we’ll tag along with Melissa while she chases big game around the world with her Winchester in hand. First we’ll head to Colorado as Melissa spot and stalks giant mule deer, then we’ll head across the pond to New Zealand to chase mountain ibex and tar, then head back to the states for Montana whitetail at ground level.
Winchester Life S3:E8 "A Grand History"

Shotgun sports are experiencing unprecedented growth, with competitions across the country gaining popularity as enthusiasts vie to be the best shooters in the world. Every summer, the Amateur Trapshooting Association hosts The Grand American in Sparta, Illinois. This year marks the 125th Anniversary for the largest trap shooting competition in the world. We’ll talk with the people who make this tournament run behind the scenes and explore what makes this tournament such a special event. We’ll also look at the rich history of The Grand. On-site we’ll chat with the curator of the Trapshooting Hall of Fame and look at some of the incredible historical pieces on display in the museum. Additionally, we’ll learn about White Flyer and how the clay manufacturer has made important innovations for the shotgun sports.
Winchester Life S3:E9 "Stahled Out"

Nobody chases the waterfowl migration as hard as Shawn Stahl of Team Winchester. The Michigan native spends over half of his year running the highways and flyways searching for the x. In this episode, we’ll tag along with Shawn and his crew with their Winchester pumps in hand as they hunker down hiding in fields and timber in hopes of locked wings from ducks and geese.
Winchester Life S3:E10 "Building The Legend"

East Alton, Illinois is the home of Winchester Ammunition and the manufacturing home of the shotshell plants that build and produce millions of rounds of ammo for hunters, sport shooters, military and law enforcement. Get an unprecedented inside look at the birth cycle of a shotshell inside the walls of Winchester. From hull to primer, shot to wad and packaging to shipping, watch how the most legendary and successful ammunition brand in the world comes to is born and built.
Winchester Life Season 1
Winchester Life Season 1
Available Now!
Airing Tuesdays at 7pm EST on the Waypoint TV Channel
Winchester Life S1:E1 "The Yackley 5"

In rural Wisconsin, we follow the Yackley family who has dedicated their lives to competitive shooting. The five-member crew is the most decorated family in shooting sports. Becky Yackley gives tips to new shooters and then teams up with Chris Keefer to review Winchester USA Ready.
Winchester Life S1:E2 "Nilo"

Over 70 years ago, John Olin took 600 acres of farmland joined by timber and turned it into a wildlife oasis in hopes of creating an ideal place to raise and train the best Labradors Retrievers in the country. He called it Nilo, which was also Olin’s model of conservation. Today, Nilo is still considered one of the most prestigious hunting and shooting preserves in the nation. This is the story of Nilo.
Winchester Life S1:E3 “The Birth of a Legend”

The story of the game changing 350 Legend cartridge is documented at deer camps across the midwest with the Keefer Brothers in Kansas, Mark and Terry Drury in Missouri, Melissa Bachman in Nebraska and Team Winchester back in Missouri.
Winchester Life S1:E4 “The Western Front”

The collaboration story between Winchester and Browning to create the groundbreaking 6.8 Western is featured. Tag along on a mule deer hunt in Utah with Chris Keefer and Winchester’s Jason Gilbertson, then follow Casey Keefer to Colorado for elk and Nebraska for whitetail where the 6.8 Western is put to the test.
Winchester Life S1:E5 “Clays and Competition”

The story of Team Winchester shooters Zach Kienbaum and Desirae Edmunds is told. Zach and Desi discuss how they got started in the shooting sports and how important coaching has become to both of them. They also talk shooting tips. Theo Ribbs breaks down the shotgun sports while Chris Keefer, Casey Keefer, Paul Sawyer, Nikki Boxler and Gabe Adair have a friendly sporting clays competition.
Winchester Life S1:E6 “Ink in the Sky”

The Pacific Northwest does not have the easiest conditions to train in for this shooting sports team. For this Seattle-based SST Ravens, this skeet and trap team finds that the challenging weather they train in comes in handy when competing. Also: Competition shooting tips form pro shooter Theo Ribbs.
Winchester Life S1:E7 “The Veteran”

Every spring brings a new opportunity to take a step forward & recall the memories of seasons past. World War II Veteran and retired Judge Arden Hill has spent most of his life chasing turkeys in the hills of Tennessee. Now in his 90’s, Arden doesn’t let age stand in the way of his love for spring turkey hunting. Also: patterning turkey shotguns and hunting spring gobblers.
Winchester Life S1:E8 "Let’s Shoot United"

Winchester introduces the Shoot United brand aimed at offering useful information and tips to the millions of new shooters entering the shooting sports. New York native and Midland University competitive shotgun shooter Amy Cawley and her journey as a student-athlete is followed. Also: Theo Ribbs gives shooting tips for competition and Foster Bartholow reviews Winchester AA Diamond Grade.
Winchester Life S1:E9 "The Rabbit Hunter"

Charles Rodney’s relationship with his hunting dogs, passion for field-to-table living and connection to the land make his love for chasing rabbits contagious while the heritage of Winchester’s small game ammunition makes this beloved pastime accessible for generations to come. Also: a Model 42 collection for the ages and hunting Kansas coyotes with the Keefer Brothers.
Winchester Life S1:E10 “Deer Season”

Deer season — the sights, the sounds and the smells stick with you. It’s about remembering those who came before us and setting the right example for those who look up to us. It’s about protecting the tradition, so they always have something to look forward to. When communities come together, we see the real reason for this special time of year.

Wildbound
Join FORLOH founder Andy Techmanski as he travels to far-flung hunting and fishing destinations in pursuit of wild game, wild meals, and the people who live by the land.
Wildbound Season 1
Wildbound Season 1
Wildbound S1:E1 "Alaska"

In this episode Andy and his friend Scotty take on Kodiak Island, Alaska for an adventure of  Salmon fishing and hunting black tail deer. Whilst traversing steep mountains in the company of Kodiak brown bears and taking on cold Alaskan waters, Andy presents useful information for fellow outdoor enthusiasts all whilst building friendships with the great people of Kodiak Big Timber Lodge.
Wildbound S1:E2 "South Africa"

In this episode Andy and his son, Logan visit the rainbow nation of South Africa. As well as hunting a wide range of African game animals, including zebra, kudu and gazelle they also dive into the Eastern Cape culinary culture. Andy also donates the game meat that he hunts to a local children shelter, he also learns about the wildlife preservation efforts that are done in the Eastern Cape.
Wildbound S1:E3 "Alabama"

In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.
Wildbound S1:E4 "UK Part 1"

In part 1 of the British episodes, Andy starts his road trip around the United Kingdom in the Cotswolds where he sets out to hunt and taste the beautiful venison that Michelin star chef, Mark Robinson has to offer. He then proceeds to travel to the border of Scotland where he samples some of the finest and most historic bird shooting practices found throughout the British Isles.
Wildbound S1:E5 "UK Part 2"

In part 2 of the British episodes, Andy visits the majestic land of Scotland. Here he is introduced to Scottish deer stalking, a type of hunting that is full of history and etiquette. He hunts ‘monarch’ stags and goes into the Atlantic waters to find some of the freshest shellfish on the planet which he, and Mike Robinson cook and eat. Concluding the British episodes in a place as wild as Scotland really sets the tone for the series.
Wildbound S1:E6 "New Zealand"

In this episode Andy and his son, Logan visit the beautiful country of New Zealand. From visiting Vineyards and sampling wine, to helicopter hunts for Himalayan Tahr, this episode is everything that is a hunters dream. As well as harvesting the wild game New Zealand has to offer Andy also learns about the importance of the conservation efforts that hunting has in this country from locals in which he spends time with.

Local Knowledge
The show Local Knowledge explores the culture of fishing itself and follows real life characters that spend their lives dedicated to fishing and the outdoors.
Local Knowledge Season 11
Local Knowledge Season 11
Local Knowledge S11:E1 "Key Largo"

Season 11 is here and we’re kicking things off where every Florida Keys adventure begins: Key Largo. In this premiere episode, Rush Maltz jumps aboard a skiff with local captain Jake Leon to explore the shallow, clear waters that make Key Largo one of the most iconic fisheries in the Florida Keys. From stealthy presentations to explosive eats, this is inshore fishing at its finest. With unmatched access to the Atlantic Ocean, Florida Bay, and Everglades National Park, Key Largo truly lives up to its reputation as the gateway to paradise. From snook and sea trout to elusive bonefish, this episode sets the tone for a season built around world-class fishing, local eats, and unforgettable destinations. Get ready for the first stop of your Florida Keys experience.
Local Knowledge S11:E2 "Islamorada"

The next stop on our Florida Keys adventure brings Rush Maltz to the legendary waters of Islamorada — a destination known for its laid-back island vibe, world-class fishing, fine dining, and easy access to the reef. Joining locals Jimmy Hendrix and Jordi Hortensi, the game plan is simple: check in by boat, chase fish, eat well, and go dive. From kite fishing the reef to fresh seafood and great company, this episode is all about experiencing the true rhythm of a Florida Keys vacation.
Local Knowledge Season 10
Local Knowledge Season 10
Local Knowledge S10:E1 "Fishing Mate"

The Odyssea Sportfishing family grows as Casey Villone comes aboard as Rush's new deck hand. While Rush gained a helping hand on his charters, he also scored a new "Fishing Mate" who is always game to bend a rod. With a day off and a report of Tuna offshore, the two head out to see if they can beat the sharks and land a Key West sushi dinner.
Local Knowledge S10:E2 "Fishing With Zeus in Key West"

Rush teams up with longtime friend and fellow angler Jesus Diaz for a well-earned "busman's holiday" in the beautiful waters of Key West. Even on a day off, these two can't resist seeing what's going to bite. Taking the wind and the tides into account, the duo maps out a strategic circuit through Key West's surrounding waters to target cobia, grouper, snapper, and tuna for a busy day "off" work.
Local Knowledge S10:E3 "SeaSafe Snook Part 1"

Water quality is everything—and the Star brite - SeaSafe team is back at it for the second year in a row! Led by Cory Redwine, Conservation Director of Project SeaSafe, this is the 4th Annual Clean Water Collective and it’s bigger than ever. This time, we’re in Grant, Florida, where 4 million clams are being released into the Indian River Lagoon, paired with the planting of thousands of mangroves, oyster work by CCA and trash clean ups to help restore one of Florida’s most critical ecosystems. But it’s not just about hard work—it’s about connection. With local captains, fishing legends, and the Local Knowledge crew on hand, we hit the water to show why this place is worth fighting for. From clam drops by drone, to in-water mangrove planting, to monster snook catches, this is hands-on conservation at its best. This two-part series kicks off with action, heart, and the kind of hope only a healthy waterway can bring. Don’t miss Part 1—where conservation meets community and everyone gets their hands wet.
Local Knowledge S10:E4 "SeaSafe Snook Part 2"

As part of this year's Star brite Sea Safe event, we hit the water to experience some of the incredible fishing the Indian River Lagoon has to offer. For Rush, that meant some serious bow time with close friend Alex Hughey - on the hunt for some of the biggest snook we've ever encountered. The bite wasn't easy, but that didn't stop Rush from smashing his personal best - not once, but twice - in one unforgettable day on the water. Don't miss this action-packed episode of the Sea Safe adventure from beautiful Sebastian, Florida!
Local Knowledge S10:E5 "Bay Day"

Join Rush and his good friend Anthony Solmo for an epic day of fishing in the stunning waters of Key West, Florida! Leaving the Odyssea at the dock, they hop on a bay boat outfitted with a trolling motor for the ultimate inshore adventure. Gliding quietly through the shallows, they target a variety of species—including hard-pulling Tarpon and explosive topwater Jacks—leaving Rush thinking, “A guy could get used to this kind of fishing!”
Local Knowledge S10:E6 "Dog Gone Yellow"

SoCal summer is calling, and the Local Knowledge crew is back on Ali’s home turf in San Diego, California. With reports of hot bites and fish moving in, the crew faces a classic Southern California dilemma — chase big yellowtail around the Coronado Islands or head north in search of bluefin tuna. The options are plenty, the challenges are real, and that’s exactly what keeps fishing in SoCal so exciting.
Local Knowledge S10:E7 "Killer Bee"

When the yellowtail at the islands refuse to play, it’s time to switch up the game plan. Ali and Rush pivot north of San Diego in search of hard-charging bluefin tuna during a multi-day mission aboard the 43’ custom ACI Catamaran "Killer Bee". Armed with the right crew, the right gear, and a never-quit mindset, the guys roll the dice on an overnight move—hoping to wake up to redemption and put a few quality bluefin on deck.
Local Knowledge S10:E8 "Moe On Mission"

Returning to Venice, Louisiana for the first time in nearly a decade, Rush reconnects with longtime friends Eric and Monique Newman of Journey South Outfitters for an unforgettable inshore fishing adventure in the heart of the Mississippi River Delta. With reports of massive schools of redfish cruising the beaches, Monique (“Moe”) must decide whether to trust the intel and make a bold run down the coast—or rely on her own instincts built from years on the water. The mission is simple: put Rush on a true bull redfish and see if he has what it takes to earn a spot in her coveted “Over 40-Inch Club.” This trip highlights why Venice, Louisiana is considered one of the premier inshore fisheries in the country—and why having the right guide makes all the difference. Target Species: Bull Redfish. Location: Venice, Louisiana – Mississippi Delta. Outfitter: Journey South Outfitters.
Local Knowledge S10:E9 "Lip Rippin' Expedition"

After a few incredible days targeting redfish on the inshore waters of Venice, Louisiana, Eric and Moe Newman bring Rush to the nearshore oil rigs — and that’s where things get wild. The rigs are holding GIANT mangrove snapper, bruiser cobia, and hard-fighting jacks… but getting them to the boat is a whole different story. Fishing heavy structure means short strikes, busted hooks, shredded leaders, and fish that know exactly where to run. Around these rigs it’s not just fishing — it’s full-blown combat. If you want to land one of these beasts, you better bring extra hooks, heavy tackle, and a whole lot of patience. Easier said than done. Venice, Louisiana continues to prove why it’s one of the most action-packed fisheries in the country.
Local Knowledge S10:E10 "Season 10 Wrap Up!"

Season 10 is a wrap! Rush takes a moment to look back on some of his favorite moments from battling giant snook and feisty tarpon in Florida, to overnighting at Catalina Island, and chasing bull redfish in Louisiana. This season was all about getting out there with good friends, making new ones, and enjoying the sport we love. An unforgettable year filled with adventure, laughs, and epic days on the water.
Local Knowledge Season 9
Local Knowledge Season 9
Local Knowledge S9:E1 "MangroveMama"

Ali and Rush head to Merritt Island and the Space Coast of Florida to participate in Star brite’s third annual SeaSafe Clean Water Collective event. Joining long time friend Cory Redwine, the guys witness the incredible mangrove planting and shoreline restoration efforts being done in the Indian river lagoon. When the work is done, it’s time to play and enjoy the resource with a quick trip on the river to fish for redfish snook and trout.
Local Knowledge S9:E2 "Permit Sketch"

With the local Key West ship wrecks covered in Permit, Rush takes good friend and marine artist KC Scott fishing for "inspiration." While permit is the plan, the boys stay rigged and ready for the numerous species the Florida Keys may deliver when fishing offshore.
Local Knowledge S9:E3 "Hooked Early"

The most important fish catch is the one that got you hooked on fishing in the first place, and for many of us that catch happened early in life. On this episode Ali and Rush grab their favorite little fishing buddies for a great day on the water on their respective coasts. In Florida, Rush spends time with his son Kade fishing for huge permit while Ali takes his nephew Duke out for some wholesome yellowtail action in California.
Local Knowledge S9:E4 "Bluefin Season GO"

As the SoCal Bluefin Tuna fishing season begins to take shape, Ali and Rush head out to fish the waters of San Diego and see what new surprises may be in store for them this year. For many boats in the fleet, the heading turns to the south for a visit to the commercial tuna pen operation that often attracts other fish from miles away. It's parking lot fishing at its best, but with a change in technique and break from the crowd Ali puts Rush on the bite!
Local Knowledge S9:E5 "United By Tuna"

When it comes to fishing and the marine industry, we are often more united by the Bluefin tuna than we think. For Ali Hussainy, the Bluefin is responsible for fueling his life long passion as an angler, and in this episode he gets to share that passion with two friends that have their own ties to the species. For wildlife biologist Forrest Galante and master sushi chef Hiroyuki Terada, catching a bluefin tuna on rod and reel will be a first!
Local Knowledge S9:E6 "War Heroes On Water"

The Local Knowledge crew heads to Catalina Island off the coast of Southern California to participate in the 2024 War Heroes On Water tournament. This event offers a unique opportunity for the sport fishing community to create meaningful connections with our nation's heroes while raising awareness of the challenges they sometimes face when transitioning back to civilian life. With a little bit of luck and a lot of teamwork, Ali and Rush hope to put their veterans on a Bluefin tuna bite they won't forget!
Local Knowledge S9:E7 "Flats Ride"

Rush takes up an invite from good friend and fellow guide Jared Cyr to sight fish for barracuda and bonefish in the shallow waters of the Florida Keys. Meanwhile Rush's boat takes a break from charter fishing to get a new Seakeeper RIDE system installed.
Local Knowledge S9:E8 "Trading Trips"

After a great trip fishing the flats, Rush returns the favor and extends an invite to friend Jarred Cyr fishing offshore. Fishing in Key West is all about the variety of species and opportunities they present. Mixing it up and taking advantage of this, the guys decide to see how many species they can catch using light spin and fly tackle offshore.
Local Knowledge S9:E9 "Return to Casa Vieja Lodge"

The LK crew returns to Guatemala with more friends for our yearly dose of world class fishing! Year after year, Casa Vieja Lodge continues to impress with daily shots at sailfish, marlin, mahi, and tuna. Tag along on our 2nd annual group trip for a tropical fishing experience like no other!
Local Knowledge S9:E10 "El Jefe"

Heavy hearts find comfort in cherished memories as the Local Knowledge crew mourns the loss of one of the greatest members of the fishing industry. Looking back on several of our episodes with our dear friend Dave Salazar, we celebrate the life of this amazing fishermen, family man, and visionary behind the world renowned Casa Vieja Lodge.
Local Knowledge Season 8
Local Knowledge Season 8
Local Knowledge S8:E1 "Cobia So Cold"

The ability to overcome obstacles and challenging conditions can be key to success, but when fishing in Key West, cold temps are rarely the hurdle. For Rush and Ali, it's another day on the water where patience and a plan pays off with Cobia and Amberjack action that gets the rods bent after a cold weather spell in the Keys.
Local Knowledge S8:E2 "Afternoon Delight"

When it comes to fishing in Key West, you can often get a lot done in a little bit of time.  After an early check in to Ocean’s Edge Resort and a mid day departure from then dock, Ali looks to Rush to put him on an afternoon bite of tuna and bottom fish that few other places can offer.  If Rush is going to deliver, he will need to make a plan and stick to it to make the best use of this limited time.
Local Knowledge S8:E3 "Watch Eric"

For Ali and Rush, one of the best aspects of fishing is being able to share experiences on the water with interesting people.  On this episode we invite luxury watch enthusiast Eric Rivera -  AKA "Watch Eric" to the Florida Keys to target Permit and Amberjack in the Gulf.
Local Knowledge S8:E4 "The Green Turtle Club Part 1"

The Local Knowledge crew FINALLY make it to the Bahamas and the incredible Green Turtle Club for a week of fishing, diving, and exploring the Abaco Island chain. With two incredible boats at our disposal, we make the 200 mile crossings from Miami and Cape Canaveral to rendezvous at Green Turtle Cay. Stay tuned for Part one of our Bahamas adventure!
Local Knowledge S8:E5 "The Green Turtle Club Part 2"

The Green Turtle Cay adventure continues as the LK crew uncovers more of what the incredible out islands of the Bahamas have to offer. Shifting gears to bottom fishing, Ali catches a fish of a lifetime while Rush gets to dive and spearfish with the locals.
Local Knowledge S8:E6 "Deep Drop Rockfish"

Back in San Diego and with Rockfish on the mind, the LK crew fish new grounds and depths to catch these iconic Southern California bottom fish. Along for the ride is pro snowboarder and friend Dave Downing to round out this epic rock cod fishing trip.
Local Knowledge S8:E7 "SoCal Small Tuna"

With the drastic changes in the Southern California fishery over the past several years, it has been easy to get fixated on targeting large Bluefin tuna and going all out on what many have coined "big fish" fishing. On this episode Ali and Rush take advantage of the many other opportunities this time of year has to offer and switch focus back to the basics, targeting smaller yellowfin, bluefin, and mahi on bait.
Local Knowledge S8:E8 "Casa Vieja Experience"

Thinking about a travel fishing trip of a lifetime? Check out what the participants of the first Local Knowledge-Casa Vieja group trip have to say about our favorite lodge in this special edition episode!
Local Knowledge S8:E9 "KeyLimey"

What do you do when you fish for a living and get a day off? You go fishing! With weather on the forecast and a last minute cancelation, Rush calls long time friend Capt. Tony "Key Limey" Murphy to see what will bite before the cold front hits Key West.
Local Knowledge S8:E10 "Mutton Or Nothing"

It might be cold and windy, but if you have trust in your guide the result could be an epic day of fishing! Rush steps up to the challenge and puts Ali on an incredible bite of mutton snapper while also checking another species off of Ali's bucket list!
Local Knowledge S8:E11 "Reflection"

As season 8 comes to a close, Ali and Rush reflect on some of the more memorable moments on the water. From the Florida Keys to San Diego, Guatemala, and the Bahamas, the show has taken us on quite the adventures with more to come!
Local Knowledge Season 7
Local Knowledge Season 7
Local Knowledge S7:E1 "Best Laid Plans"

The boys are back in the Florida Keys to kick off the new season. Ali gets to check out Rush's new digs at the Ocean's Edge Resort and take a ride on the new, Mercury powered SeaVee 34Oz. Rush has been dialing in his new live bait set up and has a few freshies to hopefully feed to a hungry wahoo. And if that doesn't work, there's always something great to catch in the Florida Keys.
Local Knowledge S7:E2 "Everglades Fish Co."

Ali and Rush head to Everglades City Florida to meet up with local Capt. Jimmy Wheeler of the Everglades Fishing Company to get a taste of what the saying "Old Florida" really means. With the SeaVee 270Z bay boat in tow, the guys look to Jimmy to put them on the bite, fishing the shallow inshore wrecks and rock piles for Snook, Grouper, and Cobia.
Local Knowledge S7:E3 "Lone Star Low Land"

Ali and Rush head to the Bay Flats Lodge in Seadrift Texas to fish the vast shallow water marshes of the San Antonio Bay for Redfish.  With thousands of acres of lowland creeks and pools to explore, this premier lodge has the tools and techniques it takes to get the guys their shots at this skinny water species.
Local Knowledge S7:E4 "The Otherside"

Ali and Rush head to Merritt Island, the sleepy space coast town where Ali was first introduced to fishing in Florida. Hoping to partake in a specialized fishery termed "fishing the other side," the guys will need calm weather, a serious boat, and a capable crew if they are going to have a shot at the yellowfin tuna that cruise the edge of the gulf stream current 100 plus miles off the coast.
Local Knowledge S7:E5 "Panama Part 1: Tucan Tuna"

In a move to scratch one off the destination bucket list, Ali and Rush reunite with old friend Juan Spragge at the Toucan Lodge in Panama's Gulf Of Chiriqui. With close access to Hannibal bank and the islands of Montuosa and Coiba, the guys target mahi, marlin and tuna while enjoying the secluded comfort and hospitality of this unique family run lodge
Local Knowledge S7:E6 "Panama Part 2: Poppin’ Tucan"

The Panama adventure continues as Ali and Rush switch their focus to the inshore possibilities at the Tucan Lodge in the Gulf Of Chiriqui. With numerous islands, pinnacles, and structures to work live baits and lures, the guys target species such as rooster fish, mullet snapper, and the king of the bottom... the Cubera Snapper.
Local Knowledge S7:E7 "Socal Foam"

The word is out! The Southern California Bluefin Tuna fishing is on fire and the waters of San Diego are literally Foaming with Life. Ali and Rush switch their targets from the jumbo size Bluefin Tuna to a smaller, medium grade fish for non-stop run and gun action. But, with so many fish fixated on the masses of bait in the area, it may take a million casts to pull one out of the froth.
Local Knowledge S7:E8 "Invite Extended"

Possibly more rewarding then an invite to experience a new fishery is one's ability to return the favor. For Ali and Rush, sharing their home waters with others is one of the best parts of the job. While Rush takes SoCal friends Brandon and Eric Nelson fishing in Key West for Permit, Ali rounds up a familiar Florida Everglades native for some San Diego Bluefin Tuna fishing.
Local Knowledge S7:E9 "Return To Baja Pt. 1"

In the first installment of this two part series, the guys are bringing it back to where it all began for Local Knowledge. Ali and Rush head to Baja California Sur to meet up with Julio Meza and friends in the towns of La Bocana and Abreojos to target large Gulf and Broomtail grouper in shallow water. This time around the crew trades the stressful drive with boat in tow for a quick flight right into grouper country.
Local Knowledge S7:E10 "Return To Baja Pt. 2"

The Baja Reunion continues as Ali and Rush fish for large shallow water groupers with good friend Julio Meza in the towns of Abreojos and La Bocana. Exploring what makes for such a strong fishery, the guys check out the nearby estuaries that serve as the birthplace for many of these grouper before heading out for a "big one."
Local Knowledge S7:E11 "Mahi Foam"

Following the Bluefin action of early summer,  Ali and Rush head back to San Diego to see what August has to offer.  With the tuna settling in and a kelp fishery in full swing, Ali considers this to be the "peak" time to target bluefin tuna, mahi, and yellowtail.  With each year before bringing a new twist to the fishery, this season follows suit with a "puddle" of Mahi that leaves the guys scratching their heads.
Local Knowledge S7:E12 "Seven Down"

With seven seasons down,  the Local Knowledge crew is looking forward to another year of fish, people, and places, but first Ali and Rush take a look back at what made season 7 a year to remember. From wade fishing Texas for Redfish to making it back to the Baja groupers that started it all, there were plenty of surprises and heartbreaks to plant this season on our binge watch list.
Local Knowledge Season 6 (2021)
Local Knowledge Season 6 (2021)
Local Knowledge S6:E1 "Wahoo or Bust"

Early winter in Key West means wahoo is on everyone’s mind and Ali and Rush are ready to put in the lures and the time to get it done.  Once the conditions line up it’s time to go all in, wahoo or bust.
Local Knowledge S6:E2 "Rockfish Taco"

Fish tacos are about as SoCal as it gets and on this visit to San Diego, Ali takes Rush fishing on a rockfish and lobster "grocery run". Slow pitch jigging and hoop netting are the techniques of choice and the rewards are delicious.
Local Knowledge S6:E3 "If This Boat Could Talk"

Ali and Rush head to Guatemala to fish with David Salazar at Casa Vieja Lodge aboard the latest addition to the fleet.  If this boat could talk, the tales of record breaking fish and world travels would be endless and Dave’s big plans for it are sure to make for an exciting new chapter.
Local Knowledge S6:E4 "Gulffish”

The boys are back in the Florida Keys fishing with Capt. Tony Murphy from the Saltwater Angler tackle shop in Key West aboard his 32’ SeaVee. Tony takes the boys into the Gulf of Mexico to chase Permit on wrecks using live crabs for bait. The Gulf doesn’t disappoint and also provides plenty of action from other Florida Keys species.
Local Knowledge S6:E5 "The Slow Pitch"

With slow pitch jigging being the newest craze, Rush and buddy Dane head out to test some new tackle and go for “mutton or nothing.”  Meanwhile in California, Ali gets his hand on the new gear and prepares to hop a flight and join in on the “slow pitch” party with the guys in Key West.
Local Knowledge S6:E6 "Tails and Teeth"

When the weather in San Diego throws the crew a curveball, Ali, Rush and friend Brandon Nelson put together plan B. Targeting Yellowtail and Mako sharks, the guys search for kelp paddies on the horizon and look to friend at the fish house for some “shark attractant” to make it all come together.
Local Knowledge S6:E7 "Shrimp Boats and New Boats"

Fishing with friend Tony Murphy in Key West, the guys head into the Gulf of Mexico to target jumbo sized Bonito behind the shrimp boats. With a brand new boat on the way, Rush hopes to stock up on his prized bait source for the shakedown trip and upcoming charters on the new rig.
Local Knowledge S6:E8 "City of Paz 1"

Rush and Ali head to La Paz Mexico to fish with longtime friends and soak up some Baja living in the City of Peace.  In part one of this two part series, the guys fish offshore targeting striped marlin and dorado with a few surprises.
Local Knowledge S6:E9 "City of Paz 2"

The La Paz adventure continues as Rush and Ali get a better understanding
of the town (and its taco spots), before heading out for some inshore fishing, targeting roosterfish and
snapper.

Rush and Ali are back in San Diego, California to fish offshore for Yellowtail and Bluefin Tuna.

With Season 6 in the can, Ali and Rush lookback on some of the places, catches, and memorable moments during this year of “new normal.”

Breaking Boundaries

"JP DeRose Breaking Boundaries" is a riveting television show that follows the exploits of renowned angler JP DeRose as he sets out on a mission to push the boundaries of fishing. In this captivating series, JP takes on daring fishing expeditions and employs innovative techniques to catch species. With adrenaline-pumping challenges, breathtaking landscapes, and educational insights, this show will inspire both fishing enthusiasts and adventure seekers to think outside the box and break through the limits of their own angling experiences.

The Reel American Road Trip

Reel American Road Trip follows our host Danny Pruitt, as he adventures around the country fishing in some of the Nation’s best kept secrets all while discovering the communities, culture, food, and the people that make each episode unique and entertaining. Accustomed to the lakes and ponds of North Georgia, Danny’s goal is to catch more than just Bass, see more than just the Pines, and eat and drink in places he never thought he would ever visit.

Turkey Posse
Wanted: Thomas L. Beard. The Turkey POSSE is hot on his trail, and they'll do anything to collect the bounty. Follow along as the POSSE rides across the country in their favorite springtime pursuit.
Turkey Posse Season 4
Turkey Posse Season 4
Turkey Posse S4:E1 "Minnesota Turkey Hunting: Season Kickoff"
Turkey hunting in Minnesota can be tough during opening week, but this season started on fire. Strutters in the decoys, hard-gobbling timber birds, fanning a solo tom … this episode has it all.
Turkey Posse S4:E2 "Turkey Hunting in Tennessee with the Pleasure Gang"

It’s opening morning of turkey season in the hills of Tennessee and we’re set up on the X. Gobblers are on fire and ready to dance. When the Turkey POSSE and the Pleasure Gang get together, we have a good time!
Turkey Posse S4:E3 "Texas Turkey Hunting: Rios Down & First Hunt Memories"

Texas turkey hunting doesn’t disappoint in this action-packed TURKEY POSSE episode. Will Cooper and his buddy Matthew Itz get it done on some fired-up Rio Grandes, with Will even doubling up on a solo mission when opportunity strikes. But the best moment comes when Matthew and Will take Matthew’s son into the field for his very first turkey hunt. From wide eyes in the blind to watching his dad bag a big tom, it’s a full-circle hunt that’s about more than just punching tags — it’s about passing it on. Rio's gobbling, close encounters, and memories that’ll last a lifetime… this one has it all.

Phillip Culpepper and the guys head to Mississippi to meet up with long time friend Perry Ross.  After a tough start, Culpepper finally finds two midday gobblers strutting on a power line.
Spring Thunder S4:E10 "Longbeard Up Close And Personal | The Redemption Gobbler"

Phillip Culpepper is back in Florida for one of the most epic three days of the season. The ups and down with friends ends with his cousin, Amy Smith, putting the dot where it needs to be right before the storm hits.

All Thing Hunting's Cole Barthel hunts some of the toughest birds in the Union in his home state of Louisiana. He is joined by friend and turkey hunting freak Tommy Huggins, and All Things Hunting's Kyle Barefield.
Spring Thunder S4:E12 "Giant Georgia Gobbler | Singletary Boys Go Hunting"

The Singletary boys are back for the annual boys' weekend with Phillip Culpepper and the Spring Thunder crew.  Ever since their dad Daryle passed away, Mercer and Jonah have been hunting the Georgia youth weekend with the guys. Check out their recent turkey hunt.
Spring Thunder S4:E13 "South Georgia Turkeys With Singletary Boys | Part 2"
The crew continues their hunt during the Georgia Youth Turkey season with Jonah and Mercer Singletary.  Mercer has busted a beak now his brother Jonah is more than eager to seal the deal.
Spring Thunder S4:E14 "The Highs And Lows Of Turkey Hunting | Youth Turkey Hunting"

David Blanton goes hunting with his long-time friend, Shawn Hollingsworth, and Shawn's son, Addox. Addox -- A.K.A. turkey slayer -- is after a gobbler on the family farm but learns that it's not always easy. Later in the hunt, Blanton hunts with Benji Hartley, and they all experience the highs and lows of turkey hunting.
Spring Thunder S4:E15 "Dropping A Strutter Out Of The Sky | Alabama Gobbler Getter"

When your nephew calls and ask you to take him hunting, you load up and head to the turkey woods. Phillip Culpepper takes his nephew and brother-in-law -- Mason and Marc Webb -- hunting in Alabama during spring break. As usual, things get western when the tom spooks and takes flight.
Spring Thunder S4:E16 "Mexico Gould's Turkey | A Bucket List Bird"

David Blanton heads to Mexico to chase his first ever Gould's turkey. The birds are flocked up, making the hunting rough, but patience pays off and Blanton marks the Gould's off the bucket list. It completes his Royal Slam.
Spring Thunder S4:E17 "A Big 'Bama Double | No Decoy, No Problem"

Phillip Culpepper and Drake Lamb kill two Alabama strutters on two separate properties in one morning. They accomplish both of these hunts without the use of a decoy. That's a pretty incredible feat. Check out this action-filled southern hunt.
Spring Thunder S4:E18 "A Mohawk Turkey? | 30-30 Youth Ministries Hunt"

It's the opening week of turkey season in Georgia. Phillip Culpepper is back with 30-30 Youth Ministries. He's hoping to call in Kaden McWhirter's first turkey on his first morning afield. The group ends up calling in a turkey with a mohawk, and that's something they have never seen before in the turkey woods.
Spring Thunder S4:E19 "Huge Spurs And Monster Bucks | Kansas Turkey Hunting Road Trip"

You will not believe the hooks on this turkey. David Blanton heads to Kansas to hunt with his good friend, Tony Hale, at JB Outfitters in Kansas.  The turkeys are acting right and David and Tony are ready to capitalize.  David is also hunting where he has taken some of his biggest bucks to date.
Spring Thunder S4:E20 "Tennessee Longbeard Double | Open Field Turkeys"
It's the first of April and the guys go to Tennessee to hunt with good friend Darrell Prentice. It's not long before Phillip Culpepper and Prentice find a big group of strutters in a big open field.
Spring Thunder S4:E21 "David Blanton Completes His Royal Slam"
David Blanton heads to Nebraska where he chases some thunder with good friends. They see Merriam's, Rio Grande turkeys, and hybrids, too. Check out the action.
Spring Thunder S4:E22 "Monster Missouri Spring Turkey | Get That Gobbler"

James? James?? James??? Realtree's 1st-year video producer James Thompson gives Drake Lamb a few days off and goes with Phillip Culpepper to Missouri. They're hunting with Mike Bray and Jay Phelps from Habit Clothing, and they're just waiting on the the word, "shoot."
Spring Thunder S4:E23 "Gobbler At The Fence?!? | Turkeys For Tomorrow Sweepstakes Hunt"

The Spring Thunder crew is in Texas for the Turkeys for Tomorrow Sweepstakes Hunt. We also take a look at what TFT has been working on this spring to improve the turkey population. To become a member of Turkeys for Tomorrow, visit www.turkeysfortomorrow.org.
Spring Thunder S4:E24 "Spring Strutters Everywhere | A Texas Turkey Pile-up"
We continue the Lone Star fun with part 2 of the Turkeys for Tomorrow sweepstakes Texas hunt. The guys battle high winds and hard-headed gobblers. But they succeed and fill plenty of tags.
Spring Thunder S4:E25 "Scouting For Turkeys Pays Off | Monster Midwestern Gobblers | Spring Thunder"

Drake Lamb returns North to hunt with his long-time friends, Alex and Mark Farver, and Justin Karns, in their home state of Iowa. They have been scouting a group of birds for the past month. Now, the 3rd season is here, and they are ready to get after them.
Spring Thunder S4:E26 "First Iowa Gobbler | Mark's Birthday Surprise"

Phillip Culpepper goes after his first ever Iowa Long Beard while hunting with Expedition's Mark Heck on his birthday.  Also, in camp Bone Collector Michael Waddell and Chris Cobbett.  Even though it is unseasonably cold for this time of the year the turkeys are still gobbling and putting on a show.
Spring Thunder S4:E27 "Maine Gobblers On The Ground | Last Leg Of The Year"

Phillip Culpepper and Drake Lamb head to Maine to hunt with their good friend, Chris Cobbett, of Element Outdoors. The 2022 turkey season is winding down, and they hope to finish with a bang ... literally. After a swing and a miss, Lamb is looking for redemption and Culpepper is hoping he doesn't get run over by two charging toms. Will they achieve their goals?

Realtree's David Blanton heads to South Florida to hunt Osceola turkeys on opening day. Blanton is hunting the Florida oak hammocks with Townsend and Sons Everglade Outfitters.
Spring Thunder S3:E4 "Culpepper Bags a Florida Gobbler | Osceola Longbeard Down | Spring Thunder"
Phillip Culpepper catches up to a big Florida gobbler of his own. The season is off to a great start. The crew is on fire.

David Blanton and Nick Mundt head to Texas to hunt Rio Grande turkeys. They cross paths with some big longbeards. Tags filled.

After Drake Lamb's successful birthday bird, Darrell Prentice is up to bat in Tennessee. Game on. He and Phillip Culpepper double up on some stubborn gobblers.

The guys meet up with Tyler Farr and Nate Hosie. Then, they call in a longbeard right off the roost. Later that day, Phillip Culpepper and Drake Lamb head to a brand new farm they just got permission to hunt, and right before fly-up, Drake Lamb cuts down a gobbler that comes a long way.

Spring Thunder S3:E23 "A First Merriam's Wild Turkey | 7J Outfitters in Wyoming | Spring Thunder"

Drake Lamb heads to Wyoming in search of his first Merriam's turkey. He meets up with Phillip Culpepper and our good friends at Seven J Outfitters. The guys are in tons of birds but have to work hard to get Lamb's first Merriam's in range. This is a hunt he won't forget.
Spring Thunder S3:E24 "In Memory of Shannon | Phillip Culpepper is Back | Spring Thunder"
After taking a break from hunting for personal reasons, Phillip Culpepper gets back in the turkey woods. They spend some time in Wyoming, and then hunt in Montana, too.

Phillip Culpepper and Drake Lamb make one last stop with some good friends at J&J Guide Service before leaving Montana. Rich turns the guys loose and they almost get it done on the first evening. Spring Thunder S3:E26 "Hunting Huge Gobblers In Maine | Late-Season Turkey Hunting | Spring Thunder"

Phillip Culpepper and Drake Lamb make the trek northeastward to Maine for the final trip of the 2021 turkey season. The guys waste no time getting on a bird but have to work a henned-up longbeard for over an hour before he presents a shot.

Realtree's Tyler Jordan and Producer Bryan Brown head southward to Florida for the opening week of the southern zone turkey season.
Spring Thunder S2:E8 "3 Gobblers, 6 Steps | Hunting with the Bone Collectors | Realtree Spring Thunder"

Spring Thunder S2:E10 "MLB Baseball Player Austin Riley Tags First Turkey | Realtree Spring Thunder"

Spring Thunder S2:E11 "Georgia Youth Turkey Hunt Prep Work | 30-30 Ministries | Realtree Spring Thunder"
The Realtree Spring Thunder crew is scouting ahead of the Georgia youth turkey hunting weekend. The crew is hunting with 30-30 Ministries. It's going to be a fun event.

Phillip Culpepper calls in another gobbler for a young hunter during Georgia's youth turkey season. John Fox is trying to get his first turkey and it doesn't take long before a big gobbler is strutting at 15 steps.
Spring Thunder S2:E14 "Best of Realtree Spring Thunder Week 1 | Florida Osceola Turkey Hunting Action"
Week No. 1 was great. Phillip Culpepper, Tyler Jordan and all of their friends had great hunts. It was a week full of funny jokes, great pranks, challenging mishaps and tagged turkeys.
Spring Thunder S2:E15 "Longbeard Gobbling Frenzy | Turkeys Across the Water | Realtree Spring Thunder"

Phillip Culpepper and Drake Lamb keep the heat on them during the last day of the Georgia youth season. It's an epic, gobble-crazed morning. Phillip Culpepper meets up with good friend and retired MLB player Ben McDonald in Mississippi. Both are avid outdoorsmen and love hunting. Naturally, they try and get Ben his first turkey. With recent college cancellations and online learning due to the COVID-19 virus, McDonald's son, Jase is home and wants to try his luck at turkey hunting, too.

After a slow morning of turkey hunting, Ben McDonald decides to wet a line and put a few bass and catfish in the boat for supper. McDonald, Phillip Culpepper and Drake Lamb head afield for a little surf and turf action.

It's playoff time in Mississippi. Ben McDonald is on a quest to experience his first “classic" turkey hunt. He wants to call one down the gun barrel while turkey hunting with his buddy Phillip Culpepper. That's exactly what they try to do. McDonald can barely hold it together when a big, fat gobbler rushes in to five steps. They almost get run over by this long-bearded Mississippi tom.
Spring Thunder S2:E19 "Tyler Jordan Scouts Alabama Turkeys | Birds Gobbling | Realtree Spring Thunder"

Tyler Jordan and Bryan Brown head over to leased land in Alabama in search of a turkey for his brother, Colton. The property is near the Chattahoochee River, and they plan to find birds for opening day. As luck would have it, they hear two birds gobbling off the roost in the exact same spot where Michael Pitts killed his opening day bird last year. They leave that spot and finding another bird gobbling across the road. Needless to say, anticipation is high for opening day.
Spring Thunder S2:E20 "Alabama Opening-Day Double | Longbeards At 10 Steps | Realtree Spring Thunder"

Tyler Jordan is joined by his little brother, Colton, on opening day of the Alabama turkey season. The day before, during a scouting trip, Tyler located a few gobblers that were roosted. Now, it's opening morning, and they slip into the area before daylight. Shortly afterward, birds start hammering. After a few yelps, three longbeards run in to the H.S. decoys.
Spring Thunder S2:E22 "Georgia Turkey Season Opening Day | Big Hen At 7 Yards | Realtree Spring Thunder"

In the first of three videos, the Spring Thunder crew heads home for opening day of the Georgia turkey season and a special hunt. Long-time Team Realtree member, avid turkey hunter, and country music singer, Daryle Singletary, passed away in 2018. His two young boys have his passion for the outdoors and get to experience opening weekend in the Georgia woods. Some of their father's old hunting buddies guide them afield.
Spring Thunder S2:E23 "Afternoon Turkey Hunting | Larry "The Cable Guy" Calls | Realtree Spring Thunder"

The Spring Thunder crew is on a quest to get Jonah and Mercer Singletary their first turkeys. The gobbling is sparse, but the laughs are plentiful. Jonah and Mercer experience firsthand that even though the hunting can be tough, a good time can still be had.
Spring Thunder S2:E24 "Gobbler Struts Into Range | Colton Jordan Turkey Hunt | Realtree Spring Thunder"

Realtree's Bill Jordan takes his son, Colton, on an afternoon hunt at Realtree Farms in Georgia. They make a move after spotting a gobbler in a green field. They work the bird, and it struts right into range. He tags his second turkey of the season and Bill couldn't be more excited. Needless to say, Colton is on a roll.
Spring Thunder S2:E25 "Singletary Boys Go Turkey Hunting | Big Longbeard Down | Realtree Spring Thunder"

In one of the most memorable hunts of the year, the late Daryle Singletary's son's, Jonah and Mercer, finally get a gobbler into range while hunting with some of their dad's old hunting buddies. Emotions run high after a weekend of tough hunting pays off. Watch Jonah and Mercer's hunt of a lifetime. "We ain't never had too much fun,"as Daryle Singletary used to say.
Spring Thunder S2:E26 "Best of Realtree Spring Thunder Week 2 | Alabama and Mississippi Hunts"

Tyler and Colton Jordan, as well as Phillip Culpepper and others, enjoy great week two hunts. From Alabama to Mississippi, they fill tags (and bass bags) like nobody's business. The turkeys are really taking it on the chin. Or should we say ... taking it on the beak. (And quite literally, we might add.)
Spring Thunder S2:E27 "Georgia Public-Land Gobbler | Midday Longbeard Luck | Realtree Spring Thunder"

Phillip Culpepper and Drake Lamb go turkey hunting on public land. Culpepper draws a coveted tag that took three years to obtain. Fortunately, the turkeys cooperate. It doesn't go without challenges, though. Wet weather puts a damper on things, but they hang in there and put themselves in the right spot. Eventually, a lunch-time tom fires up.
Spring Thunder S2:E28 "Country Music Singer Riley Green  | Big Gobbler Down | Realtree Spring Thunder"

Tyler Jordan and Riley Green try to tag two birds on Facebook Live at Realtree Farms in Georgia. Jordan roosts a group of longbeards the night before the hunt. At daylight, the team slips into the area, but it's slow at first. Finally, the group of gobblers gets fired up. Seven longbeards ease into the field and two break away to pick a fight with the H.S. decoy. Green takes the 20-yard opportunity.
Spring Thunder S2:E29 "Public-Land Longbeard Double | Georgia Turkey Hunting | Realtree Spring Thunder"

"When the peaches are ripe, you better keep picking 'em." -- Michael Waddell. That saying holds true as Phillip Culpepper and Drake Lamb head back to hunt public land again in Georgia. It's day No. 3 on the open-to-all ground and the gobblers are getting fired back up. After a couple bouts with tough longbeards, Culpepper doubles up and tags out on Georgia public land.
Spring Thunder S2:E30 "Long-Spurred Georgia Birds | Riley Green, Tyler Jordan | Realtree Spring Thunder"

After striking out during the early morning pursuit, they finally find a couple lonely toms. The birds are hot, and Tyler Jordan and Riley Green double up in the burnt Georgia pines on Realtree Farms.
Spring Thunder S2:E31 "First Southern Gobbler | Cameraman Gets to Turkey Hunt | Realtree Spring Thunder"
Spring Thunder Producer Drake Lamb gets some deserved trigger time in the South. They head across the river to Alabama in hopes of finding a willing longbeard.
Spring Thunder S2:E32 "Best of Realtree Spring Thunder Week 3 | Private- and Public-Land Turkey Hunting"
The third week of Spring Thunder was great, and the hunts reflected it. Check out the best and latest action from the turkey woods. A lot of longbeards meet their maker.
Spring Thunder S2:E34 "Father-Son Turkey Hunt | Roger and Phillip Culpepper | Realtree Spring Thunder"
Roger and Phillip Culpepper go after stubborn turkeys in Alabama. It's set to be a great hunt. Phillip is behind the camera. Roger and Producer Drake Lamb are center stage.
Spring Thunder S2:E36 "Late-Morning Alabama Gobbling Frenzy | Tyler Jordan | Realtree Spring Thunder"

Tyler Jordan and producer Bryan Brown set up on a couple gobblers early in the hunt. Then, after striking out, they find a couple hot-gobbling turkeys late in the morning. The birds gobbled on their own long enough for them to get within the toms' comfort zones. After that, a few soft yelps called them within range. Watch this hunt unfold in an eastern Alabama sweet gum thicket.
Spring Thunder S2:E37 "Mississippi Turkey Hunts | Shelter-in-Place Day No. 1 | Realtree Spring Thunder"

Phillip Culpepper and Drake Lamb arrive in Mississippi to shelter in place for the next couple of weeks. They're back at a friend's farm they hunted on a couple weeks prior. Hopefully, they'll get on more turkeys. Of course, they need something to do while the world is on lockdown.
